---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
---

# Introduction
This notebook will explore the usage of Compositional Data Analysis (CoDA) techniques in the infrared spectroscopy of protein samples.

# Data Import
```{r}
## Import the reference data
data <- read.csv("data/data.csv")
ref <- data[, c(1:5)]
spectra <- data[, c(1, 6:ncol(data))]
source("utils/preparation.R")
spectra <- prepare_df(spectra, 900, 2000)
rownames(spectra) <- spectra[,1]
spectra <- spectra[,-1]
```

# Compositional Data Analysis (CoDA) Preparation

```{r}
#import CoDA packages
library(compositions)

# replace zero on the reference data with a very small value (0.0001)
ref[ref == 0] <- 0.0001

## The total amount is meaningless or the individual amounts are part of a whole (in equal units) and the data should be analyzed in a relative geometry
y_acomp <- acomp(ref[,-1])
y_ilr <- as.matrix(ilr(y_acomp))
rownames(y_ilr) <- ref$PDB.Num

## The total amount is meaningful and the data should be analyzed in relative geometry
y_aplus <- aplus(ref[,-1])
y_ilt <- as.matrix(ilt(y_aplus))
rownames(y_ilt) <- ref$PDB.Num

## The total amount is constant, but data is treated using absolute geometry. 
y_rcomp <- rcomp(ref[,-1])
y_ipt <- as.matrix(ipt(y_rcomp))
rownames(y_ipt) <- ref$PDB.Num

## The total amount matters, and differences are treated absolutely.
y_rplus <- rplus(ref[,-1])
y_iit <- as.matrix(iit(y_rplus))
rownames(y_iit) <- ref$PDB.Num

y_coda <- list(
  "ilr" = y_ilr,
  "ilt" = y_ilt,
  "ipt" = y_ipt,
  "iit" = y_iit
)

#create an X_list
X_list <- list(
  "Full_spectra" = spectra,
  "amide_I" = spectra[, as.numeric(colnames(spectra)) >= 1600 & as.numeric(colnames(spectra)) <= 1700],
  "amide_I_II" = spectra[, as.numeric(colnames(spectra)) >= 1500 & as.numeric(colnames(spectra)) <= 1700]
)

```

# Preprocessing Spectra
```{r}
library(prospectr)
library(spectacles)

prep <- list(
    'Raw' = function(x) {x},
    'SNV' = function(x){standardNormalVariate(x)},
    '1st_Der' = function(x){savitzkyGolay(x, w = 11, porder = 2, dorder = 1)},
    '2nd_Der' = function(x){savitzkyGolay(x, w = 11, porder = 2, dorder = 2)},
    'EMSC' = function(x){
      df <- EMSC(x, degree = 2)
      return(df$corrected)
    },
    'RNV' = function(x){
      rnv(x, r = 0.25)
    }
)


``` 

## Modelling and evaluation
### PLS

```{r}
library(pls)
library(plsVarSel)
library(spectacles)

# Helper function 
calculate_cv_metrics <- function(model, y, ncomp) {
  preds_cv <- model$validation$pred[, , ncomp]
  y_mat <- as.matrix(y)
  
  RMSECV <- sqrt(mean((preds_cv - y_mat)^2))
  
  ss_res <- sum((preds_cv - y_mat)^2)
  ss_tot <- sum(scale(y_mat, scale = FALSE)^2)
  R2_CV <- 1 - (ss_res / ss_tot)
  
  RPD_CV <- sd(as.vector(y_mat)) / RMSECV
  
  return(list(R2 = R2_CV, RMSE = RMSECV, RPD = RPD_CV))
}

# Main function
pipeline <- function(X_list, y_list, max_comp = 20, k_folds = 5) {
  
  final_results <- data.frame()
  # loop through each dataset
  for (i in seq_along(X_list)) {
    # add baseline correction to the raw spectra
    X <- X_list[[i]]
    wavenumbers <- as.numeric(colnames(X))
    X_raw <- baseline(X, wavenumbers)
     for(j in seq_along(y_list)){
      y <- y_list[[j]]
      dataset_name <- paste("Dataset", i , "with Ref", j)
            
      # Loop through each preprocessing
      #preprocessing methods
      prep <- list(
          'Raw' = function(x) {
              savitzkyGolay(x, w = 15, p = 2, m = 0)},
          'SNV' = function(x){
              standardNormalVariate(savitzkyGolay(x, w = 15, p = 2, m = 0))
              },
          '1st_Der' = function(x){
              savitzkyGolay(x, w = 9, p = 2, m = 1)
              },
          '2nd_Der' = function(x){
              savitzkyGolay(x, w = 9, p = 2, m = 2)
          },
          'EMSC' = function(x){
              df <- EMSC(savitzkyGolay(x, w = 15, p = 2, m = 0), degree = 2)
              return(df$corrected)
          },
          "RNV" = function(x){
              rnv(x, r = 0.25)
          }
      )
      for (prep_name in names(prep)) {
        X_prep <- prep[[prep_name]](X_raw)
          # Loop through each variable selection method
          # variable selection methods
          varsel <- list(
              "None" = function(x){1:ncol(x)},
              "VIP" = function(x) {
                  df <- filterPLSR(y, x, ncomp = max_comp, ncomp.opt = "minimum", validation = "CV", segments = k_folds, VIP.threshold = 0.7)
                  return(df$VIP)
                  },
              "SR" = function(x) {
                  df <- filterPLSR(y, x, ncomp = max_comp, ncomp.opt = "minimum", validation = "CV", segments = k_folds, SR.threshold = 0.7)
                  return(df$SR)
              },
              "sMC" = function(x) {
                  df <- filterPLSR(y, x, ncomp = max_comp, ncomp.opt = "minimum", validation = "CV", segments = k_folds, sMC.threshold = 0.05)
                  return(df$sMC)
              },
              "UVE" = function(x) {
                  df <- mcuve_pls(y, x, ncomp = max_comp, N = 7)
                  return(df$mcuve.selection)
              }
          )
        for (vs_name in names(varsel)) {
          selected_vars <- varsel[[vs_name]](X_prep)
          
          # Skip to the next iteration if fewer than 2 variables are selected (to prevent PLS errors)
          selected_vars <- as.numeric(selected_vars)
          selected_vars <- selected_vars[!is.na(selected_vars) & selected_vars > 0]
          if (length(selected_vars) < 2) {
            next
          }
          
          # Subset the preprocessed spectra
          X_sel <- X_prep[, selected_vars, drop = FALSE]
          
          # Cap max components so it doesn't exceed the number of selected variables
          current_max_comp <- min(max_comp, length(selected_vars))
          
          # Refit PLS with the selected variables and calculate metrics
          train_data <- data.frame(y = y)
          train_data$X_sel <- I(as.matrix(X_sel))
          refit_pls <- plsr(y ~ X_sel, data = train_data,ncomp = current_max_comp, validation = "CV", segments = k_folds)
          
          # Find new optimal components for the refitted model
          rmse_cv_refit <- RMSEP(refit_pls, estimate = "CV")$val[1,1,]
          opt_comp_refit <- which.min(rmse_cv_refit[-1])
          
          # Calculate final metrics
          refit_metrics <- calculate_cv_metrics(refit_pls, y, opt_comp_refit)
          print(paste(dataset_name, "with Preprocessing:", prep_name, "and Var Selection:", vs_name, "is done"))
          # Store results
          final_results <- rbind(final_results, data.frame(
            Dataset = dataset_name, 
            Preprocessing = prep_name, 
            Var_Selection = vs_name, 
            ncomp = opt_comp_refit,
            R2_CV = refit_metrics$R2, 
            RMSECV = refit_metrics$RMSE, 
            RPD_CV = refit_metrics$RPD
          ))
        }
      }
    }
  }
  print("Pipeline execution completed!")
  return(final_results)
}

```


```{r}
sec_str_plsr <- pipeline(X = X_list, y_list = y_coda)
write.csv(sec_str_plsr, "pls_pipeline_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
save.image("pls_pipeline_results.RData")
```


## Kernel pls

```{r}
library('bigPLSR')
# Main function
bigPLSR_pipeline <- function(X_list, y_list, max_comp = 20, k_folds = 5) {
  
  final_results_bigPLSR <- data.frame()
  # loop through each dataset
  for (i in seq_along(X_list)) {
    # add baseline correction to the raw spectra
    X <- X_list[[i]]
    wavenumbers <- as.numeric(colnames(X))
    X_raw <- baseline(X, wavenumbers)
     for(j in seq_along(y_list)){
      y <- y_list[[j]]
      dataset_name <- paste("Dataset", i , "with Ref", j)
            
      # Loop through each preprocessing
      #preprocessing methods
      prep <- list(
          'Raw' = function(x) {
              savitzkyGolay(x, w = 15, p = 2, m = 0)},
          'SNV' = function(x){
              standardNormalVariate(savitzkyGolay(x, w = 15, p = 2, m = 0))
              },
          '1st_Der' = function(x){
              savitzkyGolay(x, w = 9, p = 2, m = 1)
              },
          '2nd_Der' = function(x){
              savitzkyGolay(x, w = 9, p = 2, m = 2)
          },
          'EMSC' = function(x){
              df <- EMSC(savitzkyGolay(x, w = 15, p = 2, m = 0), degree = 2)
              return(df$corrected)
          },
          "RNV" = function(x){
              rnv(x, r = 0.25)
          }
      )
      for (prep_name in names(prep)) {
        X_prep <- prep[[prep_name]](X_raw)
          # Loop through each variable selection method
          # variable selection methods
          varsel <- list(
              "None" = function(x){1:ncol(x)},
              "VIP" = function(x) {
                  df <- filterPLSR(y, x, ncomp = max_comp, ncomp.opt = "minimum", validation = "CV", segments = k_folds, VIP.threshold = 0.7)
                  return(df$VIP)
                  },
              "SR" = function(x) {
                  df <- filterPLSR(y, x, ncomp = max_comp, ncomp.opt = "minimum", validation = "CV", segments = k_folds, SR.threshold = 0.7)
                  return(df$SR)
              },
              "sMC" = function(x) {
                  df <- filterPLSR(y, x, ncomp = max_comp, ncomp.opt = "minimum", validation = "CV", segments = k_folds, sMC.threshold = 0.05)
                  return(df$sMC)
              },
              "UVE" = function(x) {
                  df <- mcuve_pls(y, x, ncomp = max_comp, N = 7)
                  return(df$mcuve.selection)
              },
              "GA" = function(x) {
                  set.seed(123)
                  df <- ga_pls(y, x, GA.threshold = 10, iters = 10, popSize = 50)
                  return(df$ga.selection)
              }
          )
        for (vs_name in names(varsel)) {
          selected_vars <- varsel[[vs_name]](X_prep)
          
          # Skip to the next iteration if fewer than 2 variables are selected (to prevent PLS errors)
          selected_vars <- as.numeric(selected_vars)
          selected_vars <- selected_vars[!is.na(selected_vars) & selected_vars > 0]
          if (length(selected_vars) < 2) {
            next
          }
          
          # Subset the preprocessed spectra
          X_sel <- X_prep[, selected_vars, drop = FALSE]
          
          # Cap max components so it doesn't exceed the number of selected variables
          current_max_comp <- min(max_comp, length(selected_vars))
          
          # PLS cross-validation with various techniques
          pls_method <- c("simpls", "nipals","kernelpls", "widekernelpls")
          for (method in pls_method){
            refit_pls <- pls_cross_validate(X = X_sel, Y = y, ncomp = current_max_comp, algorithm = method, metrics = c("rmse", "mae", "r2"), folds = k_folds)
            
            #select the lowest rmse
            best_ncomp <- refit_pls$summary$ncomp[refit_pls$summary$metric == "rmse"][which.min(refit_pls$summary$value[refit_pls$summary$metric == "rmse"])]
            best_metrics <- refit_pls$summary[refit_pls$summary$ncomp == best_ncomp, ]

            final_results_bigPLSR <- rbind(final_results_bigPLSR, data.frame(
              Dataset = dataset_name, 
              Preprocessing = prep_name, 
              Var_Selection = vs_name, 
              PLS_Method = method,
              ncomp = best_ncomp,
              R2 = best_metrics$value[best_metrics$metric == "r2"],
              RMSE = best_metrics$value[best_metrics$metric == "rmse"],
              MAE = best_metrics$value[best_metrics$metric == "mae"]
            ))
            print(paste(dataset_name, "with Preprocessing:", prep_name, "Var Selection:", vs_name, "and PLS Method:", method, "is done"))
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  print("Pipeline execution completed!")
  return(final_results_bigPLSR)
}

```
```{r}
sec_str_big_plsr <- bigPLSR_pipeline(X = X_list, y_list = y_coda)
write.csv(sec_str_big_plsr, "bigPLSR_pipeline_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
save.image("sec_structure.RData")
```

## Random Forest

```{r}
library(randomForestSRC)
library(randomForest)
library(kernlab)

rf_cv_tune <- function(X, Y, folds = 5) {
  n_samples <- nrow(X)
  n_features <- ncol(X)
  n_cols <- ncol(Y) # Capture the number of CoDA columns
  
  # Define the tuning grid for mtry
  mtry_grid <- unique(c(
    max(1, floor(sqrt(n_features))), 
    max(1, floor(n_features / 3)), 
    max(1, floor(n_features / 2))
  ))
  
  set.seed(42)
  fold_ids <- sample(rep(1:folds, length.out = n_samples))
  
  best_rmse <- Inf
  best_metrics <- list()
  
  # Loop through the grid to find the best mtry
  for (m in mtry_grid) {
    Y_pred_cv <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_samples, ncol = n_cols)
    
    # --- THE FIX: Loop through each column of the Y matrix ---
    for (c in 1:n_cols) {
      y_col <- Y[, c]
      
      for (k in 1:folds) {
        train_idx <- which(fold_ids != k)
        test_idx <- which(fold_ids == k)
        
        # Train Random Forest on the specific column
        # (I also set ntree to 500 here as we discussed!)
        rf_model <- randomForest(x = X[train_idx, , drop = FALSE], 
                                 y = y_col[train_idx], 
                                 mtry = m, ntree = 500)
        
        Y_pred_cv[test_idx, c] <- predict(rf_model, X[test_idx, , drop = FALSE])
      }
    }
    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    
    # Calculate global RMSE for this tuning parameter across all predicted columns
    Y_flat <- as.numeric(Y)
    Pred_flat <- as.numeric(Y_pred_cv)
    current_rmse <- sqrt(mean((Y_flat - Pred_flat)^2))
    
    # If this is the best tuning so far, save the metrics
    if (current_rmse < best_rmse) {
      best_rmse <- current_rmse
      rss <- sum((Y_flat - Pred_flat)^2)
      tss <- sum((Y_flat - mean(Y_flat))^2)
      
      best_metrics <- list(
        r2 = 1 - (rss / tss),
        rmse = current_rmse,
        mae = mean(abs(Y_flat - Pred_flat)),
        best_mtry = m
      )
    }
  }
  return(best_metrics)
}


rf_pipeline <- function(X_list, y_list, max_comp = 20, k_folds = 5) {
  
  final_results_RF <- data.frame()
  
  for (i in seq_along(X_list)) {
    X <- X_list[[i]]
    wavenumbers <- as.numeric(colnames(X))
    X_raw <- baseline(X, wavenumbers)
    
    for(j in seq_along(y_list)){
      y <- y_list[[j]]
      dataset_name <- paste("Dataset", i , "with Ref", j)
      
      # Preprocessing Methods
      prep <- list(
          'Raw' = function(x) { savitzkyGolay(x, w = 15, p = 2, m = 0) },
          'SNV' = function(x) { standardNormalVariate(savitzkyGolay(x, w = 15, p = 2, m = 0)) },
          '1st_Der' = function(x) { savitzkyGolay(x, w = 9, p = 2, m = 1) },
          '2nd_Der' = function(x) { savitzkyGolay(x, w = 9, p = 2, m = 2) },
          'EMSC' = function(x) { 
              df <- EMSC(savitzkyGolay(x, w = 15, p = 2, m = 0), degree = 2)
              return(df$corrected) 
          },
          "RNV" = function(x) { rnv(x, r = 0.25) }
      )
      
      for (prep_name in names(prep)) {
        X_prep <- prep[[prep_name]](X_raw)
        
        # Variable Selection / Feature Extraction Methods
        # Notice: They all now return the transformed X matrix directly!
        varsel <- list(
            "None" = function(x, y_ref) { return(x) },
            
            "VIP" = function(x, y_ref) {
                df <- filterPLSR(y_ref, x, ncomp = max_comp, ncomp.opt = "minimum", validation = "CV", segments = k_folds, VIP.threshold = 0.7)
                vars <- as.numeric(df$VIP)
                vars <- vars[!is.na(vars) & vars > 0]
                if(length(vars) < 2) return(NULL)
                return(x[, vars, drop = FALSE])
            },
            
            "GA" = function(x, y_ref) {
                set.seed(123)
                df <- ga_pls(y_ref, x, GA.threshold = 10, iters = 10, popSize = 50)
                vars <- as.numeric(df$ga.selection)
                vars <- vars[!is.na(vars) & vars > 0]
                if(length(vars) < 2) return(NULL)
                return(x[, vars, drop = FALSE])
            },
            
            "PCA" = function(x, y_ref) {
                # 1. Identify and keep only columns with actual variance
                valid_cols <- apply(x, 2, var) > 1e-10
                x_clean <- x[, valid_cols, drop = FALSE]
                
                # 2. Safety check in case too many columns were dropped
                if(ncol(x_clean) < 2) return(NULL)
                
                # 3. Run PCA safely on the clean data
                pca_mod <- prcomp(x_clean, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
                return(pca_mod$x[, 1:min(max_comp, ncol(pca_mod$x)), drop = FALSE])
            },
            
            "Kernel_PCA" = function(x, y_ref) {
                # 1. Identify and keep only columns with actual variance
                valid_cols <- apply(x, 2, var) > 1e-10
                x_clean <- x[, valid_cols, drop = FALSE]
                
                if(ncol(x_clean) < 2) return(NULL)
                
                # 2. Run Kernel PCA on the clean data
                kpca_mod <- kpca(~., data = as.data.frame(x_clean), kernel = "rbfdot", features = max_comp)
                return(rotated(kpca_mod))
            }
          )
        
        for (vs_name in names(varsel)) {
          
          # Pass both X_prep and y (needed for VIP and GA)
          X_sel <- varsel[[vs_name]](X_prep, y)
          
          # Skip if the selection method failed or returned too few variables
          if (is.null(X_sel) || ncol(X_sel) < 2) {
            next
          }
          
          # Call the new Random Forest Cross-Validation and Tuning function
          rf_metrics <- rf_cv_tune(X = X_sel, Y = y, folds = k_folds)
          
          final_results_RF <- rbind(final_results_RF, data.frame(
            Dataset = dataset_name, 
            Preprocessing = prep_name, 
            Var_Selection = vs_name, 
            Model_Method = "Random_Forest",
            Best_Mtry = rf_metrics$best_mtry,
            R2 = rf_metrics$r2,
            RMSE = rf_metrics$rmse,
            MAE = rf_metrics$mae
          ))
          
          print(paste(dataset_name, "| Prep:", prep_name, "| Extractor:", vs_name, "| Tuned RF is done!"))
        }
      }
    }
  }
  print("Pipeline execution completed!")
  return(final_results_RF)
}
```


```{r}
sec_str_rf <- rf_pipeline(X_list = X_list, y_list = y_coda)
write.csv(sec_str_rf, "rf_pipeline_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
save.image("sec_structure.RData")
```


```{r}
# 1. Define only the packages needed for the XGBoost pipeline
xgb_packages <- c(
  "xgboost",     # The core machine learning algorithm
  "compositions",# For your ternary CoDA math (ilr, ilt, etc.)
  "baseline",    # For your initial baseline correction
  "prospectr",   # For Savitzky-Golay, SNV, and derivatives
  "EMSC",        # For your EMSC preprocessing block
  "plsVarSel",   # For the VIP and Genetic Algorithm (GA) extractors
  "kernlab"      # For the Kernel PCA extractor
)

# 2. Loop through and install/load automatically
for (pkg in xgb_packages) {
  # If the package is missing, install it
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
    message(paste("Installing missing package:", pkg))
    install.packages(pkg, dependencies = TRUE)
  }
  
  # Load it into the environment
  library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
}

message("All XGBoost pipeline packages are installed and loaded!")

xgb_cv_tune <- function(X, Y, folds = 5) {
  n_samples <- nrow(X)
  n_cols <- ncol(Y)
  
  # A streamlined tuning grid perfect for spectroscopy
  tune_grid <- expand.grid(
    max_depth = c(3, 6),       # Shallow trees prevent overfitting the noise
    eta = c(0.05, 0.1),        # Slower learning rates are generally safer
    nrounds = c(100, 200)      # Max iterations
  )
  
  set.seed(42)
  fold_ids <- sample(rep(1:folds, length.out = n_samples))
  
  best_rmse <- Inf
  best_metrics <- list()
  
  # Loop through the grid
  for (i in 1:nrow(tune_grid)) {
    params <- tune_grid[i, ]
    Y_pred_cv <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_samples, ncol = n_cols)
    
    # Loop through each column of the CoDA Y matrix
    for (c in 1:n_cols) {
      y_col <- Y[, c]
      
      for (k in 1:folds) {
        train_idx <- which(fold_ids != k)
        test_idx <- which(fold_ids == k)
        
        # XGBoost requires explicit matrix conversion
        dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data = as.matrix(X[train_idx, , drop = FALSE]), label = y_col[train_idx])
        dtest <- xgb.DMatrix(data = as.matrix(X[test_idx, , drop = FALSE]))
        
        # Train the model
        xgb_model <- xgb.train(
          params = list(
            objective = "reg:squarederror",
            max_depth = params$max_depth,
            eta = params$eta
          ),
          data = dtrain,
          nrounds = params$nrounds,
          verbose = 0 # Keeps your console clean!
        )
        
        Y_pred_cv[test_idx, c] <- predict(xgb_model, dtest)
      }
    }
    
    # Calculate global metrics
    Y_flat <- as.numeric(Y)
    Pred_flat <- as.numeric(Y_pred_cv)
    current_rmse <- sqrt(mean((Y_flat - Pred_flat)^2))
    
    # Save if it is the best one
    if (current_rmse < best_rmse) {
      best_rmse <- current_rmse
      rss <- sum((Y_flat - Pred_flat)^2)
      tss <- sum((Y_flat - mean(Y_flat))^2)
      
      best_metrics <- list(
        r2 = 1 - (rss / tss),
        rmse = current_rmse,
        mae = mean(abs(Y_flat - Pred_flat)),
        best_depth = params$max_depth,
        best_eta = params$eta,
        best_nrounds = params$nrounds
      )
    }
  }
  return(best_metrics)
}

xgb_pipeline <- function(X_list, y_list, max_comp = 20, k_folds = 5) {
  
  final_results_XGB <- data.frame()
  
  for (i in seq_along(X_list)) {
    X <- X_list[[i]]
    wavenumbers <- as.numeric(colnames(X))
    X_raw <- baseline(X, wavenumbers)
    
    for(j in seq_along(y_list)){
      y <- y_list[[j]]
      dataset_name <- paste("Dataset", i , "with Ref", j)
      
      # Preprocessing Methods
      prep <- list(
          'Raw' = function(x) { savitzkyGolay(x, w = 15, p = 2, m = 0) },
          'SNV' = function(x) { standardNormalVariate(savitzkyGolay(x, w = 15, p = 2, m = 0)) },
          '1st_Der' = function(x) { savitzkyGolay(x, w = 9, p = 2, m = 1) },
          '2nd_Der' = function(x) { savitzkyGolay(x, w = 9, p = 2, m = 2) },
          'EMSC' = function(x) { 
              df <- EMSC(savitzkyGolay(x, w = 15, p = 2, m = 0), degree = 2)
              return(df$corrected) 
          },
          "RNV" = function(x) { rnv(x, r = 0.25) }
      )
      
      for (prep_name in names(prep)) {
        X_prep <- prep[[prep_name]](X_raw)
        
        # Variable Selection / Feature Extraction Methods
        varsel <- list(
            "None" = function(x, y_ref) { return(x) },
            
            "VIP" = function(x, y_ref) {
                df <- filterPLSR(y_ref, x, ncomp = max_comp, ncomp.opt = "minimum", validation = "CV", segments = k_folds, VIP.threshold = 0.7)
                vars <- as.numeric(df$VIP)
                vars <- vars[!is.na(vars) & vars > 0]
                if(length(vars) < 2) return(NULL)
                return(x[, vars, drop = FALSE])
            },
            
            "GA" = function(x, y_ref) {
                set.seed(123)
                df <- ga_pls(y_ref, x, GA.threshold = 10, iters = 10, popSize = 50)
                vars <- as.numeric(df$ga.selection)
                vars <- vars[!is.na(vars) & vars > 0]
                if(length(vars) < 2) return(NULL)
                return(x[, vars, drop = FALSE])
            },
            
            "PCA" = function(x, y_ref) {
                valid_cols <- apply(x, 2, var) > 1e-10
                x_clean <- x[, valid_cols, drop = FALSE]
                if(ncol(x_clean) < 2) return(NULL)
                pca_mod <- prcomp(x_clean, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
                return(pca_mod$x[, 1:min(max_comp, ncol(pca_mod$x)), drop = FALSE])
            },
            
            "Kernel_PCA" = function(x, y_ref) {
                valid_cols <- apply(x, 2, var) > 1e-10
                x_clean <- x[, valid_cols, drop = FALSE]
                if(ncol(x_clean) < 2) return(NULL)
                kpca_mod <- kpca(~., data = as.data.frame(x_clean), kernel = "rbfdot", features = max_comp)
                return(rotated(kpca_mod))
            }
        )
        
        for (vs_name in names(varsel)) {
          
          X_sel <- varsel[[vs_name]](X_prep, y)
          
          if (is.null(X_sel) || ncol(X_sel) < 2) {
            next
          }
          
          # Call the new XGBoost Cross-Validation and Tuning function
          xgb_metrics <- xgb_cv_tune(X = X_sel, Y = y, folds = k_folds)
          
          final_results_XGB <- rbind(final_results_XGB, data.frame(
            Dataset = dataset_name, 
            Preprocessing = prep_name, 
            Var_Selection = vs_name, 
            Model_Method = "XGBoost",
            Best_Depth = xgb_metrics$best_depth,
            Best_Eta = xgb_metrics$best_eta,
            Best_Nrounds = xgb_metrics$best_nrounds,
            R2 = xgb_metrics$r2,
            RMSE = xgb_metrics$rmse,
            MAE = xgb_metrics$mae
          ))
          
          print(paste(dataset_name, "| Prep:", prep_name, "| Extractor:", vs_name, "| Tuned XGBoost is done!"))
        }
      }
    }
  }
  print("XGBoost Pipeline execution completed!")
  return(final_results_XGB)
}
```


```{r}
sec_str_xgb <- xgb_pipeline(X_list = X_list, y_list = y_coda)
write.csv(sec_str_xgb, "xgb_pipeline_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
save.image("sec_structure.RData")
```
